description: ESDE 10 Axes x 48 Levels (Layer 3-4). English definitions.

temporal.name: Temporal Condition
temporal.definition: The pattern of how things exist, change, or maintain their state over time.

emergence.definition: The moment when something first appears or comes into being

indication.definition: The stage where signs of change appear but have not yet fully manifested

influence.definition: The stage where the emerged thing begins to exert effects on its surroundings

transformation.definition: The stage where things undergo qualitative change due to influence

establishment.definition: The stage where the transformed state stabilizes and settles

continuation.definition: The stage where the established state persists

permanence.definition: The stage of enduring existence beyond time, permanence

scale.name: Spatial/Scale Condition
scale.definition: The scope and scale at which a phenomenon is observed and exerts influence.

individual.definition: The scope of a single individual's inner world and actions

community.definition: The scope of direct relationships: family, neighborhood, groups

society.definition: The scope of social institutions, nations, cultural spheres

ecosystem.definition: The scope of ecosystems and global environment

stellar.definition: The scope of planetary and stellar systems

cosmic.definition: The scope of the entire cosmos, the fundamental scale of existence

epistemological.name: Epistemological Condition
epistemological.definition: The process by which consciousness apprehends, assigns meaning, and arrives at new understanding.

perception.definition: The stage of apprehending existence through the senses

identification.definition: The stage of distinguishing and naming what has been perceived

understanding.definition: The stage of grasping the meaning and structure of what has been identified

experience.definition: The stage of integrating understanding into oneself beyond mere comprehension

creation.definition: The stage of generating new cognition and concepts from experience

ontological.name: Ontological Condition
ontological.definition: The layers of which an entity is composed — its very constitution.

informational.definition: The layer of information and data

relational.definition: The layer of relationships with other entities

structural.definition: The layer of patterns and structures

semantic.definition: The layer of meaning and value

interconnection.name: Interconnection Condition
interconnection.definition: The dynamic process by which multiple entities influence each other and deepen their relationships.

independent.definition: The stage of existing independently from other entities

catalytic.definition: The stage where contact with another entity triggers the opportunity for change

chained.definition: The stage where influence propagates as a chain of cause and effect

synchronous.definition: The stage where multiple entities change synchronously

resonant.definition: The stage where entities deeply resonate with each other

resonance.name: Resonance Depth Condition
resonance.definition: The qualitative depth to which a connection or relationship touches the essence.

structural.definition: Correspondence or similarity at the structural level

essential.definition: A deep connection that touches the essence

existential.definition: A fundamental connection involving existence itself

symmetry.name: Symmetry Relation Condition
symmetry.definition: The interactions produced by opposing or complementary symmetric forces.

destructive.definition: A relationship where symmetric forces negate or destroy each other

inclusive.definition: A relationship where one subsumes or absorbs the other

transformative.definition: A relationship where interaction between both produces qualitative transformation

generative.definition: A relationship where something new is generated from symmetric forces

cyclical.definition: A relationship where symmetric forces alternate cyclically

lawfulness.name: Lawfulness Condition
lawfulness.definition: The nature of the rules governing a phenomenon: simple causality, complex emergence, or fundamental necessity.

emergent.definition: Laws that emerge from complex systems and are difficult to predict

contingent.definition: Laws that arise from chance or conditional dependencies

necessary.definition: Laws arising necessarily from the very foundation of existence

experience.name: Experiential Quality Condition
experience.definition: The qualitative character of how a cognition manifests as an experience for consciousness.

discovery.definition: The experience of encountering the unknown

creation.definition: The experience of bringing something new into being

comprehension.definition: The experience of arriving at the comprehension of existence (ultimate understanding)

value_generation.name: Value Generation Condition
value_generation.definition: The criteria by which an entity or act is deemed to have value, progressing from utility through aesthetics and ethics to resonance with primordial existence.

functional.definition: Value based on utility and usefulness

aesthetic.definition: Value based on sensibility and aesthetic awareness

ethical.definition: Value based on social norms and moral duty

sacred.definition: Value based on resonance with primordial existence 'Aru' (Aruism neologism)
